categories.website: Specific to GIDF 2026 — teachers, schedule, dance styles, Shoonya
categories.easy: Easy general knowledge — Indian dance + Bollywood basics
categories.hard: Difficult expert knowledge — deep history, intricate styles, challenging Bollywood

questions.q: How many master teachers are featured at GIDF 2026?
questions.fact: GIDF's tagline says it all: "Three days. Seven master teachers. One community that keeps coming back."

questions.q: Which faculty member at GIDF 2026 teaches Kuthu and Desi-Afro?
questions.fact: Desihop is a collective of three artists: Gokul Gopi, Saumya Singh, and Rayeez Mohammed.

questions.q: Which folk dance does Manasi Khanna teach at GIDF 2026?
questions.fact: Giddha is the high-energy women's folk dance of Punjab — bouncier and more verbal than Bhangra.

questions.q: Where is Shoonya Dance Centre located?
questions.fact: Stapelplein 41, 9000 Gent — about 700m from Gent-Dampoort station.

questions.q: What is the name of GIDF's morning warm-up ritual?
questions.fact: Rise & Riyaaz kicks off Saturday and Sunday mornings — riyaaz means "practice" in Hindi.

questions.q: What's the GIDF community-break ritual called?
questions.fact: Spill the Chai is a 20-minute hangout in the bar area between intense workshops.

questions.q: Which closing ritual ends GIDF on Sunday afternoon?
questions.fact: "One Last Gupshup" — one last chat — wraps GIDF on Sunday 17:20–18:00.

questions.q: Whose medley is featured as Act #17 in the GIDF 2026 Gala Showcase?
questions.fact: The Madhuri Dixit Medley is performed by GIDF students — one of 18 acts on Saturday night.

questions.q: How many acts are in the GIDF 2026 Gala Showcase?
questions.fact: 18 acts, no interval, ~90 minutes — paced like a single live set.

questions.q: What time does the GIDF 2026 Gala Showcase begin on Saturday?
questions.fact: Saturday 20:30 in Shoonya Theatre. ~90 minutes, no interval.

questions.q: Who is the curator of GIDF?
questions.fact: Swapnil Dagliya curates GIDF and co-founded Shoonya Dance Centre.

questions.q: Which dance form is Vaishali Sagar's "Bamboo Beats" workshop based on?
questions.fact: Cheraw is a traditional bamboo dance from Mizoram — Vaishali teaches it Sunday 15:50–17:20.

questions.q: Who teaches Lavani at GIDF 2026?
questions.fact: Swapnil leads Lavani Sunday 13:30–15:30 — the semi-classical folk form of Maharashtra.

questions.q: Which faculty member is the live tabla maestro for GIDF 2026?
questions.fact: Hameed Khan Kawa is an internationally renowned tabla maestro who accompanies workshops with live music.

questions.q: Which workshop opens GIDF 2026 on Friday afternoon?
questions.fact: Friday 12:00–16:00: Technique & Tools Lab in Studio Aakash with Colleena Shakti.

questions.q: Which Shoonya studio doubles as the Gala Theatre?
questions.fact: Studio Art Deco is 240m² on the 1st floor — also known as Shoonya Theatre during the gala.

questions.q: Which Shoonya studio has the sprung floor?
questions.fact: Studio Aakash on the 2nd floor has the sprung floor — easier on knees, perfect for Technique & Tools.

questions.q: Where is the GIDF Opening Party held?
questions.fact: Friday 19:30–23:00 in Studio Ananta — Dariya Live Band + DJ swapkebolly.

questions.q: Who's DJing the GIDF 2026 Opening Party?
questions.fact: DJ @swapkebolly — that's Swapnil himself behind the decks.

questions.q: Which workshop does Shampa Gopikrishna lead Saturday morning?
questions.fact: Mayurbhanj Chhau on Saturday 11:00–12:30 — a martial-rooted dance from tribal Odisha.

questions.q: Friday's late-evening costume styling workshop is based on which folk form?
questions.fact: Friday closes with Kalbeliya costume styling and a community Kalbeliya class with Swapnil.

questions.q: Saturday afternoon at GIDF, Colleena Shakti teaches a workshop in which discipline?
questions.fact: Abhinaya — the expressive/storytelling layer of classical Indian dance — Saturday 15:20–16:50.

questions.q: Sunday morning at GIDF features Indian Fusion ____. Fill in the blank.
questions.fact: Indian Fusion Bellydance with Colleena, Sunday 09:30–11:00 — fuses Raqs Sharqi with Indian rhythms.

questions.q: What is the size of Studio Art Deco at Shoonya?
questions.fact: Studio Art Deco is 240m² — Shoonya's largest space and home to the gala.

questions.q: Who organises GIDF?
questions.fact: ABC a Bollywood Company organises GIDF; Swapnil curates.

questions.q: On which dates does GIDF 2026 run?
questions.fact: GIDF 2026 runs 1–3 May at Shoonya Dance Centre, Ghent.

questions.q: Which transport stop is closest to Shoonya?
questions.fact: Gent-Dampoort is ~700m from Shoonya. Bus lines 5 and 8 also stop nearby.

questions.q: Who are the three artists behind the Desihop collective?
questions.fact: Desihop = Gokul Gopi, Saumya Singh, and Rayeez Mohammed — Desi-Afro fusion specialists.

questions.q: Which dance does Shampa teach on Friday evening?
questions.fact: Shampa's Garba Choreography is Friday 18:30–20:00 — warm-up for the Opening Party.

questions.q: On which day does Manasi Khanna's Giddha workshop take place?
questions.fact: Saturday 13:30–15:00 — Giddha is the bouncier women's counterpart to Bhangra.

questions.q: Bharatanatyam originates from which Indian state?
questions.fact: Bharatanatyam is the classical dance of Tamil Nadu, originally performed in temples.

questions.q: Bhangra is the traditional folk dance of which state?
questions.fact: Bhangra began as a harvest dance in Punjab and went global through the diaspora.

questions.q: Garba originates from which Indian state?
questions.fact: Garba is danced during Navratri around a central lamp or image of the goddess.

questions.q: What is Madhuri Dixit's iconic Bollywood nickname?
questions.fact: From the song "Dhak Dhak Karne Laga" in Beta (1992) — the name stuck for life.

questions.q: Which city is the home of Bollywood?
questions.fact: Bollywood is the Hindi-language film industry centred in Mumbai (formerly Bombay → "B" + Hollywood).

questions.q: AR Rahman won an Oscar for music in which film?
questions.fact: Two Oscars in 2009 for Slumdog Millionaire — Best Original Score and Best Original Song ("Jai Ho").

questions.q: How many dance forms are officially recognised as classical by India's Sangeet Natak Akademi?
questions.fact: Eight: Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Kathakali, Kuchipudi, Manipuri, Mohiniyattam, Odissi, Sattriya.

questions.q: Madhuri Dixit's blockbuster Devdas was released in which year?
questions.fact: Devdas (2002) — directed by Sanjay Leela Bhansali, with SRK and Aishwarya Rai.

questions.q: Which classical dance form is famous for its elaborate face make-up and originates from Kerala?
questions.fact: Kathakali's makeup (chutti) can take 3–4 hours to apply before a performance.

questions.q: Lavani is the folk and semi-classical dance of which state?
questions.fact: Lavani's powerful percussion-driven rhythm is set to the Dholki drum.

questions.q: Kuchipudi is the classical dance of which state?
questions.fact: Kuchipudi takes its name from the village of Kuchipudi in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: Odissi originates from which Indian state?
questions.fact: Odissi is famous for its sculpturesque tribhanga (three-bend) posture.

questions.q: Which Hindu deity is known as "Lord of Dance" (Nataraja)?
questions.fact: Shiva as Nataraja performs the cosmic dance of creation and destruction (Tandava).

questions.q: Madhuri Dixit was extensively trained in which classical dance form?
questions.fact: She trained under the legendary Pt. Birju Maharaj — most visibly in Devdas's "Maar Daala".

questions.q: In which film did Madhuri Dixit perform the iconic song "Choli Ke Peechhe"?
questions.fact: Khal Nayak (1993) — the song was a national talking point, sparking debate about lyrics.

questions.q: In Hum Aapke Hain Koun (1994), Madhuri's leading man was…?
questions.fact: Salman Khan and Madhuri Dixit — directed by Sooraj Barjatya, one of the highest-grossing Hindi films of the 90s.

questions.q: What are the bell anklets worn by classical Indian dancers called?
questions.fact: A typical Kathak dancer's ghungroos can carry 100+ bells per ankle.

questions.q: Which classical form is famous for fast spins (chakkars) and rhythmic footwork?
questions.fact: Kathak emerged from temple storytellers — the kathakas — and absorbed Mughal court influences.

questions.q: Mohiniyattam is the classical dance of which state?
questions.fact: Mohiniyattam means "dance of the enchantress" — performed in Kerala's traditional white-and-gold sari.

questions.q: Who composed India's national anthem, "Jana Gana Mana"?
questions.fact: Tagore wrote it in 1911 — and also composed Bangladesh's anthem, the only person to write two national anthems.

questions.q: Which legendary musician is most associated with the sitar internationally?
questions.fact: Pandit Ravi Shankar brought the sitar to a global audience — including teaching George Harrison of The Beatles.

questions.q: Which legendary tabla virtuoso has performed with Mickey Hart, Yo-Yo Ma, and George Harrison?
questions.fact: Zakir Hussain is widely regarded as one of the greatest tabla players ever to live.

questions.q: In which Madhuri Dixit film was the chart-topping song "Ek Do Teen" featured?
questions.fact: Tezaab (1988) — choreographed by the legendary Saroj Khan; the song made Madhuri an overnight sensation.

questions.q: Which dance is associated with Krishna and his cowherd lover-companions (gopis)?
questions.fact: Manipuri Raas Leela — gentle, graceful, and devotional, with elaborate skirted costumes.

questions.q: What is Shah Rukh Khan widely known as in Bollywood?
questions.fact: Also called King Khan or Badshah of Bollywood — global superstar since the 90s.

questions.q: Sattriya is the classical dance form of which state?
questions.fact: Sattriya emerged in 15th-century Vaishnava monasteries in Assam, and was recognised as classical only in 2000.

questions.q: Manipuri classical dance is from which state?
questions.fact: Manipuri is famed for its soft, controlled movements and elaborate Krishna Raas costumes.

questions.q: What is the colour-throwing Indian festival called?
questions.fact: Holi marks the arrival of spring and celebrates the victory of good over evil.

questions.q: Saroj Khan was India's most celebrated dance ____.
questions.fact: Saroj Khan choreographed many of Madhuri's iconic songs, including "Ek Do Teen" and "Dola Re Dola".

questions.q: Which Hindi term refers to a stand-alone, often glamorous song-and-dance number not tied to the plot?
questions.fact: An "item number" is a Bollywood staple — designed to be a self-contained showpiece.

questions.q: Which is the only Chhau style traditionally performed without masks?
questions.fact: Mayurbhanj Chhau (Odisha) is maskless. Seraikella (Jharkhand) and Purulia (West Bengal) both wear masks.

questions.q: Pt. Birju Maharaj — Madhuri Dixit's classical guru — belonged to which Kathak gharana?
questions.fact: The Lucknow Gharana is known for its grace, expressiveness (bhav), and storytelling lineage.

questions.q: Lavani is traditionally divided into which two main types?
questions.fact: Phadachi Lavani is performed for live theatre crowds; Baithakichi Lavani is the seated, classical version.

questions.q: In which year was Kalbeliya inscribed on the UNESCO Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity?
questions.fact: 2010 — Kalbeliya is performed by the Kalbeliya/Sapera (snake-charmer) community of Rajasthan.

questions.q: Who founded the Kalakshetra dance institute, reviving Bharatanatyam in the 20th century?
questions.fact: Rukmini Devi Arundale founded Kalakshetra in 1936 in Madras (Chennai).

questions.q: The Tanjore Quartet were 19th-century brothers credited with codifying which classical form?
questions.fact: The four Tanjore brothers — Chinnayya, Ponnayya, Sivanandam, and Vadivelu — structured the modern Bharatanatyam margam (recital order).

questions.q: Bharata Muni is the legendary author of which foundational Indian performing arts treatise?
questions.fact: The Natya Shastra (~200 BCE–200 CE) is the encyclopaedic source-text for Indian classical dance, drama, and music.

questions.q: How many single-hand gestures (asamyukta hastas) are described in classical Indian dance theory?
questions.fact: 28 asamyukta hastas (single-hand) and 24 samyukta hastas (combined-hand) — a vocabulary of expressive mudras.

questions.q: The classical Bharatanatyam concept of "Tandava" represents which energy?
questions.fact: Tandava is masculine/cosmic (associated with Shiva); Lasya is feminine/graceful (associated with Parvati).

questions.q: Cheraw — the bamboo dance — is the traditional folk form of which Indian state?
questions.fact: Cheraw (Mizoram) is performed using long bamboo poles clapped rhythmically while dancers step between them.

questions.q: Sangeet Natak Akademi — India's national academy for music, dance, and drama — was founded in which year?
questions.fact: Founded in 1953, it formally recognises which dance forms are "classical".

questions.q: In Devdas (2002), Madhuri Dixit's iconic dance solo "Maar Daala" was choreographed in which classical style?
questions.fact: Pure Lucknow-gharana Kathak — choreographed by Madhuri's guru Pt. Birju Maharaj.

questions.q: In Devdas (2002), "Dola Re Dola" featured Madhuri Dixit alongside which co-star?
questions.fact: Madhuri and Aishwarya Rai performed the duet — directed by Sanjay Leela Bhansali.

questions.q: How many Filmfare Best Actress awards has Madhuri Dixit won?
questions.fact: Four wins: Dil (1991), Beta (1993), Hum Aapke Hain Koun (1995), and Devdas (2003).

questions.q: Madhuri Dixit was awarded the Padma Shri by the Government of India in which year?
questions.fact: Padma Shri in 2008 — India's fourth-highest civilian award.

questions.q: Which 2007 film marked Madhuri Dixit's comeback to lead Bollywood roles after a five-year break?
questions.fact: Aaja Nachle (2007) — directed by Anil Mehta. Madhuri played a NY-returned dancer reclaiming a hometown amphitheatre.

questions.q: Madhuri Dixit was born in which year?
questions.fact: Born 15 May 1967 in Mumbai.

questions.q: Madhuri Dixit's 2019 song "Tabaah Ho Gaye" appeared in which Sanjay Leela Bhansali production?
questions.fact: Kalank (2019) — Madhuri performed a powerful Mujra-inspired classical solo.

questions.q: Which is the principal percussion instrument of Lavani performance?
questions.fact: Lavani is built around the high-pitched Dholki — a small two-headed barrel drum.

questions.q: Yakshagana — a centuries-old folk theatre and dance form — comes from which Indian state?
questions.fact: Yakshagana is a coastal Karnataka tradition combining dance, music, dialogue, and elaborate make-up.

questions.q: Pandit Ravi Shankar (sitar) famously taught and influenced which member of The Beatles?
questions.fact: George Harrison studied sitar with Pt. Ravi Shankar — sparking a wave of Indian-classical influence in 1960s pop.

questions.q: The signature "three-bend" sculptural posture of Odissi is called…?
questions.fact: Tribhanga — bends at the neck, waist, and knee — gives Odissi its sculpturesque silhouette.

questions.q: Sattriya classical dance was historically practiced in which kind of institution?
questions.fact: Sattriya was preserved by male monks (bhakatas) in the Sattras of Assam for ~500 years.

questions.q: The Kalbeliya dance is associated with which traditional community of Rajasthan?
questions.fact: The Sapera/Kalbeliya were snake handlers; their dance imitates serpentine movements.

questions.q: In Bharatanatyam recital structure (margam), which item is performed first?
questions.fact: The recital opens with Alarippu ("the blossoming") — a pure-rhythm invocation, before Jatiswaram and Varnam.

questions.q: Which Bollywood film's hit "Pinga" featured Madhuri-style classical fusion (though performed by other actresses)?
questions.fact: Bajirao Mastani (2015) — Deepika and Priyanka performed a Lavani-Marathi-fusion duet, very much in the Devdas "Dola Re" lineage.

questions.q: The legendary cabaret-style dancer-actress of 1960s–80s Bollywood — known by a single name — was…?
questions.fact: Helen Ann Richardson Khan starred in 700+ films, defining the Bollywood "item" tradition before the term existed.

questions.q: Which classical form's costume traditionally features a half-skirt that opens into a fan when the dancer holds a Mandi posture?
questions.fact: Bharatanatyam's stitched silk costume opens into a fan-pleat when the dancer sits in Aramandi/Mandi.

questions.q: The annual Khajuraho Dance Festival is held against the backdrop of temples in which state?
questions.fact: Khajuraho's 10th–11th-century temples (Madhya Pradesh) host one of India's most prestigious open-air dance festivals every February.

questions.q: Giddha is traditionally performed without which element that defines Bhangra?
questions.fact: Giddha is voice-and-clap driven — bolian (couplets) and handclaps — without the Dhol that anchors Bhangra.

questions.q: Mayurbhanj Chhau, Seraikella Chhau, and Purulia Chhau are linked to which broad cultural region?
questions.fact: All three Chhau styles arose along the eastern tribal belt — distinguished by mask use, technique, and lineage.

questions.q: Which classical dance form was originally codified almost entirely as a male-only tradition?
questions.fact: Kathakali was historically performed only by men — including for female roles — and remains male-dominated, though women now perform too.

questions.q: The dance term "Adavu" specifically refers to…?
options: A basic step / movement unit in Bharatanatyam
questions.fact: Adavus are the building blocks of Bharatanatyam — like the alphabet of the dance, drilled for years before composition.

questions.q: Which film ushered in Madhuri Dixit's blockbuster era — making her a top-tier star — alongside the song "Ek Do Teen"?
questions.fact: Tezaab (1988) directed by N. Chandra was Madhuri's breakout — and Saroj Khan's choreography made the song a national craze.

questions.q: Rukmini Devi Arundale's revival of Bharatanatyam was partly a response to the 20th-century stigma against which traditional community of dancers?
questions.fact: Devadasis — temple dancers — were stigmatised under colonial laws; Rukmini Devi reframed and presented the form on the proscenium stage.

questions.q: Which legendary female Kathak dancer was famously known as "the Empress of Kathak"?
questions.fact: Sitara Devi (1920–2014) — Rabindranath Tagore reportedly called her "Nritya Samragyi" (Empress of Dance).

questions.q: The folk dance Ghoomar — known for its slow circular swirling — is most strongly associated with which state?
questions.fact: Ghoomar is the signature folk dance of the Bhil and Rajput communities of Rajasthan — performed in flowing ghagras.

questions.q: Pt. Birju Maharaj choreographed and appeared in a brief role in which 2002 Bollywood film?
questions.fact: He choreographed "Maar Daala" and "Kaahe Chhed Mohe" for Devdas, working closely with Madhuri Dixit.

questions.q: The Bollywood term "playback singer" refers to whom?
options: An off-screen vocalist whose voice is lip-synced by an actor on screen
questions.fact: Playback recording lets the actor focus on performance while specialist singers (Lata Mangeshkar, Kishore Kumar, etc.) provide the voice.

questions.q: Mudras (hand gestures) are catalogued in classical Indian dance into how many primary categories?
questions.fact: Two principal categories: Asamyukta (single-hand, 28) and Samyukta (combined-hand, 24) — together a vocabulary of dozens of named gestures.
